metadata.description: An identity directory server providing LDAP and (optionally) Kerberos. Grounded in RFC 4511/4512 (LDAP protocol + information model) and RFC 4120 (Kerberos). This is the directory SERVER (a Security resource); the identity DATA it holds (Person/Group/ServiceAccount) is the Identity information category. A suite like FreeIPA realizes this PLUS Network.DNSZone and (future) Security.CertificateAuthority.

context.purpose: Models the directory server — LDAP and optionally Kerberos — that identities authenticate against and services bind to.
context.plain_description: The identity directory as a running server: which protocols it serves (LDAP, Kerberos), its realm and base DN, and its role in a replication topology (a replica depends on its primary). Consumers get endpoints once realized — the LDAP URL, the Kerberos KDC, the base DN to bind under. The server is distinct from the identity data in it: people, groups, and service accounts are their own records; a suite like FreeIPA realizes this server plus DNS zones.
use_when: You need services and hosts that authenticate against the directory to depend on it, so it stops last among them.
You need the directory replication topology (primary/replica) explicit for recovery planning.
not_for: The identities inside — Identity.Person / Identity.Group / Identity.ServiceAccount.
The DNS zones a directory suite serves — Network.DNSZone; related, but its own record.
works_with: Compute.VirtualMachine / Compute.BareMetalHost — where the directory runs.
Identity.Group — external groups sourced from this directory.
Software.Service — services requiring the directory, with hard/soft strength.
Network.DNSZone — zones served when DNS is directory-integrated.

protocols.description: Directory access protocols served (RFC 4511 LDAP; RFC 4120 Kerberos).

realm.description: Kerberos realm / identity realm, e.g. EXAMPLE.COM.

base_dn.description: LDAP base DN, e.g. dc=example,dc=com (RFC 4512 naming).

replication_role.description: Topology role within the directory cluster.

kdc_endpoint.description: Kerberos KDC endpoint, when Kerberos is served.

base_dn.description: Realized base DN consumers bind under.

relationships.name: vm_host
relationships.description: The VM the directory server runs on.

relationships.name: metal_host
relationships.description: The bare-metal host the directory server runs on.

relationships.name: replication_peer
relationships.description: Replication peers (a replica depends_on its primary/peers).

adopts.role: LDAP protocol + directory information model vocabulary (RFC 4511/4512)

aliases.name: Identity Management
aliases.note: FreeIPA/IdM (the suite realizes this + DNSZone + CA)
